rationale: Both reach the same core diagnosis (bisected to #4834's intentional level-2-sleep guard, propose switching the test to level=1) and both overreach by asserting a 'close' disposition the thread never confirms — the last visible comment (Flink-ddd) is actually a request to trigger a full sleep-mode CI run, implying the issue is still open pending verification, not resolved. X goes further off the rails by claiming the fix is 'already applied on main,' a specific factual claim with zero support in the given thread. Y is more honest (frames it as a proposed one-line fix rather than a merged fact), explicitly grounds its answer in Flink-ddd's actual CI-trigger request via a 'Thread Grounding' section, and adds a relevant completeness note about the skipped test_coordinated_cross_device carrying the same latent issue.
